behavior_summary: This script implements the in-game behavior for a generic potion item. When the player 'looks' at the item it shows the short description "potion ".

The usable behavior (U8P_POTION::use) reads the item's frame (Item::getFrame) and dispatches to multiple effect variants: empty/no-effect, two different HP-affecting branches (heals or reduces HP depending on caps), mana restoration (capped by Int*2), direct damage via a hit wrapper, a temporary berserk/enrage sequence, a sleep/stasis sequence with spawned visuals, and a status-toggle effect.
Most effect branches clear/consume the potion by calling Item::setFrame(0, this) after applying the effect. Several branches add a small randomized component (urandom) to vary amounts.

Timed or multi-step behaviors (berserk in U8P_POTION::func059B, timed status in U8P_POTION::func0687, sleep sequence in U8P_POTION::use) U8P_POTION::use spawn to create helper processes, suspend loops for timing, and stat-derived durations computed from Npc::getInt or Npc::getStr (commonly multiplied by 2).

Audio/visual feedback is provided by playSFX and spawned visual processes; some branches make the target temporarily immortal (Npc::setImmortal / Npc::clrImmortal) or put the avatar into stasis (setAvatarInStasis) during multi-step effects.
notable_control_flow: Primary dispatch is by Item::getFrame(this) inside U8P_POTION::use; each integer frame selects a distinct effect branch.

Multi-step effects U8P_POTION::use spawn to create visual/audio/timer processes and suspend to yield between steps; duration loops increment a local counter and compare it against a threshold derived from Npc stats (Int*2 or Str*2).
U8P_POTION::func059B implements a looped timed period that sets immortality at start and clears it after a counter exceeds Npc::getInt(target) * 2.
U8P_POTION::func0687 tests item status bits (0x04 and 0x10 via Item::getStatus) and either toggles a flag immediately or enters a timed toggle/untoggle loop when the 0x10 bit is present.
Most branches perform cleanup/consumption by calling Item::setFrame(0, this) to clear the potion's frame.
open_questions: Exact semantics of the item status bits checked in U8P_POTION::func0687 (0x04 and 0x10): which in-game status flags do they represent (inferred as a toggleable status effect, but concrete meaning is unknown).
Precise actor target(s) for every branch — analysis assumes the player/target NPC/avatar context, but some branches may operate on different bound entities depending on how the item is used.

Whether clearing the item frame (Item::setFrame(0)) fully destroys/consumes the potion in inventory or only marks it visually empty (metadata implies consumption but exact lifecycle beyond frame clear is not shown).
Exact mapping of SFX/visual spawn IDs to in-game animations/effects — SFX ids were observed but their user-visible labels are not present.

main_function.name: U8P_POTION::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the short description/inspection text for the potion when the player examines it.
entry_conditions: Player triggers a U8P_POTION::look/examine action on the potion item (U8P_POTION::look process type 0x0208).

function_map.name: U8P_POTION::use
function_map.role: U8P_POTION::use/activate handler — dispatches potion effects based on Item::getFrame

function_map.name: U8P_POTION::func059B
function_map.role: implements the temporary 'I feel like fighting!' berserk/enrage sequence with immortality and a timed loop

function_map.name: U8P_POTION::func0687
function_map.role: implements a status-toggle effect (plays SFX, toggles status flags, optionally timed by Int*2)

internal_calls.purpose: Invoke the berserk/enrage multi-step helper for the frame that produces an 'I feel like fighting!' effect.
Invoke the status-toggle helper for the frame that applies or toggles a timed status effect.

external_calls.purpose: Read the potion's variant/state to decide which effect branch to execute.
Clear/consume the potion by setting its frame to 0 after most effects.
Query and adjust HP and U8P_POTION::use Strength-derived caps (Str*2) for healing/damage branches.
Query and restore mana with an Int-derived cap (Int*2) in the mana-restore branch.
Apply immediate damage/hit to the target for the damaging potion variant.
Generate small random offsets to vary heal, mana, and damage amounts.
Put the avatar into stasis during the sleep potion multi-step visual/timer sequence.
Spawn auxiliary processes for animations, visual effects, and timing for multi-step effects.
Temporarily make the target immortal during the berserk period and clear it after the timed loop.
Check item status bits and toggle an in-game status flag on the target; if bit 0x10 is set the toggle is timed using a duration derived from Npc::getInt.
Play sound effects to signal effect start/end or transitions in multi-step sequences.
Yield the process or pause between spawned steps and during timing loops.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: This script implements the in-game behavior for a generic potion item. When the player 'looks' at the item it shows the short description "potion ".
